name: tasklist-tool
description: A VS Code extension for structured task and artifact management for AI agents.

languageModelTools.name: list_tasks
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve a list of tasks from the workspace. Use this when you need to see available tasks, check their status, or find a specific task ID to work on. Accepted status values: 'open', 'in-progress', 'closed'. Omit to list all tasks. By default (if parentTaskId is omitted), only top-level tasks/projects are returned.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists tasks in the current workspace, optionally filtered by status.

status.description: Optional status to filter tasks by.

parentTaskId.description: Filter tasks by parent project ID. If omitted, only top-level tasks are returned.

languageModelTools.name: create_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create a new task in the workspace with status 'open'. The taskId must be unique and URL-friendly (e.g. 'feature-login'). A task with type: 'project' acts as a container for subtasks. Use list_tasks to check for existing IDs before creating.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Creates a new task with a unique ID.

taskId.description: A unique identifier for the task (e.g., 'feature-login-implementation').

type.description: Whether this is a 'task' or a 'project'. Defaults to 'task'.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. The parent must be a task with type: 'project'.

languageModelTools.name: activate_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Set a specific task as the 'active' task for the current workspace session. Use this so that subsequent artifact operations default to this task without needing to provide the taskId every time. Only one task can be active at a time; activating a different task replaces the previous active task. If the task is a subtask, provide its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Sets a task as the active task for the workspace.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to activate (e.g. 'feature-login').

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

activateProject.description: Whether to also activate the parent project in the root index if this is a subtask. Defaults to true.

languageModelTools.name: deactivate_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clear the currently active task. Use this when you are finished working on the active task but haven't necessarily closed it yet, or when switching context away from task management.

languageModelTools.name: start_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Transition a task from 'open' to 'in-progress'. Use this when you actually begin working on a task. A task must be in 'open' status to be started. This is a primary state transition that signals active development. If the task is a subtask, provide its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Moves a task from 'open' to 'in-progress' status.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to start.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

languageModelTools.name: close_task
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Transition a task from 'in-progress' to 'closed'. Use this when all work and documentation (artifacts) for the task are complete. A task must be in 'in-progress' status to be closed. This is the final state in the task lifecycle. If the task is a subtask, provide its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Moves a task from 'in-progress' to 'closed' status.

taskId.description: The ID of the task to close.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

languageModelTools.name: list_artifact_types
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List all registered artifact types and their associated templates. Use this to discover what kind of documentation you can create (e.g., 'implementation-plan', 'walkthrough'). Returns type IDs, display names, and descriptions.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists all available artifact types and templates.

languageModelTools.name: list_artifacts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List artifact files associated with a specific task. If taskId is not provided, it defaults to the currently active task. If the task is a subtask, providing its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' is recommended for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lists artifacts for a specific task (defaults to active).

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

languageModelTools.name: get_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve the content of a specific artifact for a task. If the artifact file already exists, it returns the current content. If it doesn't exist, it returns the default template for that artifact type so you can fill it in. Defaults to active task if taskId is omitted. If the task is a subtask, providing its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' is recommended for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reads an artifact's content or returns its template if it doesn't exist.

artifactType.description: The ID of the artifact type (e.g. 'research').

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

languageModelTools.name: update_artifact
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Create or overwrite an artifact file with new content. Use this to save your research, plans, or walkthroughs. The content should be the full Markdown body (no frontmatter). Defaults to active task if taskId is omitted. If the task is a subtask, providing its parent project ID via 'parentTaskId' is recommended for strict scoping.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Creates or updates an artifact file with provided content.

artifactType.description: The ID of the artifact type.

content.description: The full Markdown content for the artifact.

taskId.description: The ID of the task. Defaults to the active task if omitted.

parentTaskId.description: Optional ID of the parent project. If the task is a subtask, providing this is required for strict scoping.

languageModelTools.name: register_artifact_type
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Register a new custom artifact type and template at the workspace level. Use this when you need a standardized document structure that isn't provided by default. Custom types are saved to '.tasks/templates/' and are available immediately.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Registers a new custom artifact type and template.

id.description: A unique ID for the custom artifact type.

displayName.description: Human-friendly name for the artifact type.

description.description: Description of what this artifact should contain.

templateBody.description: Optional Markdown template body for the new type.

languageModelTools.name: promote_to_project
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Converts an existing task into a project, enabling it to contain subtasks.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Promotes a task to a project.

taskId.description: ID of the task to promote to a project.

viewsWelcome.contents:
Tasklist Tool requires an open workspace folder. Please open a folder to manage tasks.
[Open Folder](command:revealFileInOS)

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
scripts.bundle: mkdir -p templates && cp -r ../core/src/templates/* templates/ && esbuild src/extension.ts --bundle --outfile=out/extension.js --external:vscode --platform=node --target=node20
